ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT PNEUMO ABEX LLC, SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO ABEX CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. NO ARGUMENT WAS MADE AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE REGARDING EXPOSURE TO "AMERICAN BRAKEBLOK" BRAKE LININGS. THE INTERROGATORY RESPONSES OF PNEUMO ABEX CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER PNEUMO ABEX MANUFACTURED "AMERICAN BRAKEBLOK" BRAKE LININGS AND WHETHER THE LININGS CONTAINED ASBESTOS DURING THE RELEVANT TIME PERIOD. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION AND ITS INTERROGATORY RESPONSES ARE OVERRULED. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE AND STRICT LIABILITY IS DENIED FOR SAME REASONS. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR CONCERT OF ACTION AND FRAUD, DECEIT/NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION/CONCEALMENT IS GRANTED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF DOES NOT DISPUTE THE NON-EXISTENCE OF ANY TRIABLE FACTS WITH RESPECT TO THESE CAUSES OF ACTION. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AN ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437C(G). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: SUSAN J. LEE, CSR # 4280

Off Calendar Jan-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Thomas J. Moses, Esq. of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / opposing party PNEUMO ABEX LLC, successor in interest to Abex Corporation
Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Alvin Matthews

RULING:
Defendant Pneumo Abex LLC, successor in interest to Abex Corporation's motion for summary judgment. No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is denied. Plaintiff's declaration creates a triable issue regarding exposure to "American Brakeblok" brake linings. The interrogatory responses of Pneumo Abex create a triable issue whether Pneumo Abex manufactured "American Brakeblok" brake linings and whether the linings contained asbestos during the relevant time period. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration and its interrogatory responses are overruled. Summary adjudication as to causes of action for negligence and strict liability is denied for same reasons. Summary adjudication as to causes of action for concert of action and fraud, deceit/negligent misrepresentation/concealment is granted because plaintiff does not dispute the non-existence of any triable facts with respect to these causes of action. Prevailing party is to prepare an order pursuant to CCP 437c(g). Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Susan J. Lee, CSR # 4280
